title: WTI Oil eases after three-day advance as Hormuz tensions keep supply risks in focus
body: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) declines on Thursday, trading around $101.45 at the time of writing, down 3.70% on the day after three consecutive days of gains. Despite this technical pullback, US Crude remains above the psychological $100 level, reflecting a market that is still under strain.

title: Dow Jones Industrial Average rallies above 49,500 on Caterpillar earnings boost
body: The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed sharply on Thursday, adding about 730 points, or 1.5%, after rallying from an overnight low below 48,500 to a session high just above 49,600.

title: USD/CAD falls as Dollar weakens on possible Japan FX intervention
body: USD/CAD trades on the back foot on Thursday as renewed weakness in the US Dollar (USD) supports the Canadian Dollar (CAD), while the latest US economic data fails to provide support to the Greenback. At the time of writing, the pair is trading around 1.3612, down nearly 0.53% on the day.

title: BoE recap: An “active hold” as inflation risks re-emerge
body: The Bank of England (BoE) kept rates unchanged at 3.75%, but unlike a passive pause, this was framed as a deliberate and active policy choice.

title: EUR/GBP slips as ECB and BoE hold rates, but hawks stay alert now
body: The EUR/GBP falls some 0.16% during the day as the central bank bonanza ends with the ECB and the BoE keeping interest rates unchanged as expected. The cross-pair trades at around 0.8644 after reaching a daily high of 0.8667.

title: AUD/USD rises amid robust Chinese business activity, weaker US Dollar
body: The AUD/USD recovers toward the 0.7190 price region on Thursday, erasing Wednesday's losses, as the Australian Dollar (AUD) benefits from data showing a resilient Chinese economy and a weaker US Dollar (USD).

title: Silver Price Analysis: XAG/USD rebound loses momentum amid restrictive policy risks
body: Silver (XAG/USD) moves higher on Thursday, trading around $73.40 at the time of writing, up 2.81% on the day, after undergoing a marked pullback in recent weeks. This technical rebound comes as the white metal attempts to stabilize following the rejection near the $80 threshold in mid-April.

title: Gold firms as USD weakens, higher-for-longer rates limit upside
body: Gold (XAU/USD) edges higher on Thursday, recovering from the one-month low of $4,510 seen the previous day. The modest rebound comes as the US Dollar (USD) weakens after Tokyo ramps up FX intervention warnings.

title: ECB sees two rate hikes if Brent stays above $100 – Reuters
body: Sources speaking with Reuters revealed that the European Central Bank (ECB) policymakers are likely to hike rates “at least” twice this year, beginning in June if there’s no resolution to the Iran conflict.

title: ECB: Stagflation risks complicate policy path – ING
body: ING’s Global Head of Macro, Carsten Brzeski, notes that the European Central Bank (ECB) kept interest rates on hold as stagflationary pressures in the Eurozone rise. The bank highlights weaker Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth, mixed inflation dynamics and tighter credit conditions.

title: Fed: Dots and easing path steady – ABN AMRO
body: ABN AMRO’s US Senior Economist Rogier Quaedvlieg reviews the latest Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) decision, noting that the Federal Reserve (Fed) kept rates unchanged and maintained an easing bias in its statement.

title: ECB Recap: Between rising inflation risks and a slowing economy
body: As widely telegraphed, the European Central Bank (ECB) kept its policy rates unchanged on Thursday, but the tone of the meeting reflected a far more complicated backdrop.

title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Profit-taking kicks in after fresh intervention warnings
body: The USD/JPY pair builds on the previous day's breakout momentum beyond the 160.00 psychological mark, hitting a fresh high since July 2024 on Thursday.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD looks vulnerable amid inflation fears, hawkish Fed hold
body: Gold is fading its bounce from monthly lows of $4,510 set on Wednesday, as buyers lack conviction amid renewed haven demand for the US Dollar (USD)

title: Thanks for your service, Mr. Chair Powell
body: Powell’s departure is probably the most troublesome end of a Federal Reserve (Fed) Chair, despite being the most resolute leader a central bank could have. Indeed, Powell could be labeled as conservative.

title: Why Gold price faces critical positioning trap ahead of the Fed decision
body: The Federal Reserve’s upcoming interest-rate decision is key for Gold’s price.

title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Eyes 160.00 intervention level amid US-Iran tensions, ahead of Fed
body: The USD/JPY pair looks to build on the previous day's bounce from sub-159.00 levels, or a one-week low, and climbs back closer to the overnight swing high during the first half of the European session on Wednesday.

title: Gold Price Forecast: Can Fed Chair Powell rescue XAU/USD buyers?
body: Gold is consolidating the previous sell-off to monthly lows of $4,555 set on Tuesday, as traders turn to the sidelines and refrain from placing fresh bets ahead of the Federal Reserve (Fed) monetary policy decision due later on Wednesday.

title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Bullish potential intact as traders await Fed/BoE policy meetings
body: The GBP/USD pair extends the previous day's retracement slide from the 1.3575 area, or over a one-week top, and attracts some follow-through selling on Tuesday.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD looks to Fed verdict for next big break amid stalled US-Iran talks
body: Gold is trading listlessly near the $4,700 level in Asian trading on Tuesday, maintaining its familiar range, as focus now shifts toward the two-day Federal Reserve (Fed) monetary policy meeting starting later in the day.

title: RAF troops shot down more than 100 Iranian drones and missiles fired at Middle East base
body: A coalition base in Iraq that British forces are helping to defend was targeted by around 28 Iranian-linked drones and missiles a day as the US and Israel attacked Iran, it has emerged.

title: Saudi Arabia pulls LIV Golf funding - full statement
body: Saudi Arabia's sovereign wealth fund is withdrawing investment from LIV Golf after the 2026 season, Sky News has been told.

title: Drug kingpin jailed after extradition from Dubai
body: A gangster who played a "key" role in a multi-million-pound drugs operation has been jailed for six years after being extradited to Scotland from the United Arab Emirates.

title: Trump threatens to withdraw troops from Germany
body: Donald Trump has threatened to remove US troops from Germany after the country's chancellor said America was being "humiliated" by Iran.

title: White supremacist jailed for deadly mosque shooting fails to overturn guilty plea
body: The man jailed for life after murdering 51 people at two mosques in New Zealand has lost an attempt to withdraw his guilty pleas.
